description: Classical and modern optics, including imaging, polarization optics, optical telescopes, optical microscopes, interference and interferometers, optical fibers and fiber-optic communication, optical resonators, lasers, optical modulators, introduction to quantum and nonlinear optics. Concepts covered in lecture reinforced by weekly laboratory experiments.

optimized_prerequisites.prerequisites_text: (PHYSICS 202,208, or248) and (PHYSICS 322or concurrent enrollment), or graduate/professional standing

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: (PHYSICS 202,208, or248) and (PHYSICS 322or concurrent enrollment), or graduate/professional standing
